title: Typestate Pattern
description: Encode state transitions in the type system so that invalid state transitions are compile-time errors. Each state is a separate type, and methods are only available in the appropriate state. This moves runtime checks to compile time, making illegal states unrepresentable.
whenToUse: When objects have distinct states with different valid operations
When you want to prevent calling methods in the wrong order
Protocol implementations, connection state machines, build pipelines
When the cost of a runtime error is high and you want compile-time guarantees
realWorldUse: hyper's HTTP response builder (must set status, then headers, then body). Diesel's query builder (type changes as you add clauses). TLS handshake state machines. File handles (open vs. closed).
